lead.title: Andy Burnham Becomes Labour Leader and Is Set to Be Britain’s Prime Minister
lead.snippet: Mr. Burnham promised more public control of essential services, a shift of power toward local governments and a push for economic growth.

items.title: The 20-Somethings Running Ukraine’s War Machine
items.snippet: A new generation has been cutting red tape to speed weapons to the front. But the old guard pushed back, and now Ukraine’s young defense minister is out.

items.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Hits Bridges and a Control Tower in Country’s South
items.snippet: The U.S. military’s Central Command said the latest round of attacks had “hit dozens of Iranian military targets” but made no mention of civilian infrastructure.

items.title: ‘Suddenly, I Was Free’: A Chinese Pastor’s Journey From Jail to the U.S.
items.snippet: In his first interview since being released by China, Pastor Ezra Jin Mingri details his surprise release after a direct appeal by President Trump to Xi Jinping.

items.title: How Andy Burnham, ‘King of the North,’ Conquered U.K. Politics
items.snippet: A plain-spoken politician from a modest background in northwest England, Mr. Burnham became Labour leader — and Britain’s incoming prime minister — on Friday.

items.title: Beijing Rejects Trump’s Claim of Meddling, but Avoids a Fight
items.snippet: Chinese officials called the election-meddling accusation baseless, while analysts said Beijing was trying to preserve a fragile détente.

items.title: Venezuela Earthquakes Force a Grim Homecoming for Migrants
items.snippet: Mass migration, which defines modern Venezuela, has heavily marked the earthquake, too, saving many who had left, but also making their grief harder.

items.title: Cuban Dissident Is Missing A Week After Prison Release
items.snippet: A Cuban artist awaiting permission from the Trump administration to enter the United States is being held in an unknown location in Cuba.

items.title: Hundreds of Rohingya Feared Drowned at Sea
items.snippet: Members of the persecuted minority group from Myanmar set off in search of a better life weeks ago, but news of their boats sinking only emerged recently. More than 500 are feared dead.

items.title: As Blazes Spread Near Paris, Extreme Heat Is Changing How France Fights Fires
items.snippet: Until recently, France’s forest fires were mostly in the south. This week, fires are raging much farther north. France is having to rethink its firefighting strategy.

items.title: Dubai Migrant Worker Layoffs Reveal Costs of Middle East War
items.snippet: The Persian Gulf city attracted migrant workers with the promise of opportunity. Many now say the war has brought job losses and salary cuts.

items.title: A Russian Political Battle Ends With a Visit From Masked Men
items.snippet: Boris B. Nadezhdin, an outspoken opponent of the war in Ukraine, says he knows why he was detained: “Among our leadership, there is panic and chaos.”

items.title: Philippines Condemns AI Video Posted by Chinese State Media Depicting It as a Monkey
items.snippet: It lodged a diplomatic complaint with Beijing over an A.I.-generated video centered on territorial disputes in the South China Sea.

items.title: How Andy Burnham Becomes Britain’s New Prime Minister
items.snippet: Andy Burnham is set to officially become the next prime minister on Monday, but a number of things have to happen before then. Here’s how everything is expected to play out.

items.title: 7.3-Magnitude Earthquake in Central America Sets Off Tsunami Threat
items.snippet: A powerful earthquake near the border of Mexico and Guatemala raised fears of a tsunami and shook buildings across three countries.

lead.title: FBI arrests man accused of using Steam games to drain victims’ crypto wallets
lead.snippet: Prosecutors accused 21-year-old student Zyaire Wilkins of publishing on Steam several fake video games that contained malware, infecting thousands of victims, and stealing crypto from some of them.

items.title: Parents want safer phones for kids. These companies are answering the call.
items.snippet: As parents look for alternatives to unrestricted smartphones, a growing number of companies are building phones designed specifically for kids, from feature-limited mobile devices to minimalist home phones.

items.title: Amazon fixing bug that billed some AWS customers billions of dollars
items.snippet: Some Amazon customers logged on Friday to a surprise bill estimate claiming that they owed the tech and cloud giant billions in fees.

items.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
items.snippet: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.

items.title: reMarkable’s new Paper Pure is good. That’s why I wrote this review on it.
items.snippet: Remarkable's Paper Pure replaces Remarkable 2, and it is quite good.

items.title: No product? No problem. This Disrupt 2026 session shows how to get pre-seed funding with conviction, storytelling
items.snippet: It’s not just you: AI startups are taking in a huge amount of seed funding, and in the process making things harder for anyone looking for funding even at a pre-seed stage. We’ve covered the trend in detail, and at this year’s TechCrunch Disrupt event, we want to help pre-seed fo

items.title: Zoox issues software recall after a robotaxi got confused by heavy smoke
items.snippet: The recall comes as the top automotive safety regulator in the U.S. has warned AV companies about their vehicles interfering with first responders.

items.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
items.snippet: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

items.title: San Francisco mayor pushes for tougher rules after the Waymo traffic fiasco
items.snippet: In the wake of a massive hours-long gridlock event, San Francisco mayor Daniel Lurie has told state regulators it's time to put more requirements on robotaxi operators like Waymo.

items.title: SpaceX suddenly aborts second Starship V3 launch after ignition
items.snippet: The company didn't immediately say what went wrong. SpaceX's stock plunged more than 4% in after-hours trading before paring losses.

items.title: Coca-Cola suspended production at its Fairlife dairy after a ransomware attack
items.snippet: Coca Cola said dairy production at its Fairlife unit will "remain suspended" in the United States following a hack.

items.title: Founders Fund hires former OpenAI exec Ryan Beiermeister (and not because of her ‘Mafia’ skills)
items.snippet: Ryan Beiermeister, who demonstrated cool analysis in the Founders Fund YouTube series "Mafia," has joined the firm as a partner.

items.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
items.snippet: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

items.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
items.snippet: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

items.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
items.snippet: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20-year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

items.title: Ο «Γκάρφιλντ» των βατράχων: Γλυκός, στρουμπουλός και πάντα με ύφος κριτή όμως απειλείται

lead.title: My 5 Favorite Places for Art in Venice

items.snippet: A New Jersey couple helped scientists uncover the makeup of an unusual asteroid after it fell into their bedroom.

items.title: ‘Heartstopper Forever’: What to Know Before Seeing the Movie
items.snippet: The Netflix series and its feature-length finale are based on the graphic novels by Alice Oseman. Here’s a reminder of the events of the characters and where their stories left of.
